title: Collectivization
signalsLead: This page is for you if
signals: 👉 you want to go beyond the produce/consume/sleep status quo
👉 you are looking for meaning and depth in your actions
👉 something feels stuck for you in conventional activism, conventional nonprofit work, or conventional entrepreneurship
👉 in short, you feel out of sync with your environment
missionP1: Tope-là's mission is to enable people in culture, art, craft, ecology, education, and non-medical care to find the tools, materials, and skills they need through exchange, barter, or gifting.
missionP2: Behind the apparent simplicity of this mission lies a major challenge: both stepping outside dominant market logics and creating new ones that make resource circulation smoother.
opalLeadP1: A strong path toward these new restorative logics for a cohesive social fabric lies in a still uncommon type of organization, sometimes called an "Opal Organization".
opalLeadP2: The pillars of such an organization are:
pillars: 🪄 Self-management: teams manage their work and make decisions without traditional managers, relying on consultation with peers and experts (the advice process).
🪄 Wholeness: encouraging collaborators to be themselves (emotions, values, aspirations), fostering authenticity and reducing social "masks" at work.
🪄 Evolutionary purpose: the organization is seen as a living organism that adapts and evolves around a collective mission rather than a fixed goal.
postPillarP1: Tope-là would like to transform into an "Opal" organization. In practical terms, we use meeting and collaboration processes that respect these pillars. We are not afraid to step off the beaten path, nor to use more classic tools. It always depends on what feels right for the collective mission, along with each person's sense of coherence.
postPillarP2: The only real requirement to enter this atmosphere is to give these processes a fair chance to build shared trust in the collective.
mindsetLead: But we would not be honest if we did not mention some essential posture elements:
mindset: 🎯 You approach conflict as an opportunity to discover something the collective needs to fulfill its mission
🎯 You can live with a greater amount of uncertainty than in more familiar organizational models, and see uncertainty as territory to explore, not only as a systematic source of danger
🎯 You have worked enough on your wounds to approach situations while accompanying your emotions and feelings rather than being their slave - and of course we are not machines
🎯 You have experience with one or another form of shared governance, or at least theoretical knowledge and interest
skillsLead: In terms of skills, here is a list - not necessarily exhaustive - of what Tope-là needs:
skills: 🧩 User experience guardians: for ergonomics, ease of use, intuitiveness, and the sound functioning of our creations
🧩 Spokespeople: communicate the messages Tope-là wants to share, through the internet and sometimes traditional media
🧩 Visual team: bring Tope-là's digital tools and messages to life visually, and maintain coherence that strengthens all the qualities of our creations
🧩 Ambassadors: talk about Tope-là, gather feedback, observe how Tope-là is perceived, sense how to better reach our audience, and maintain a relationship with users
🧩 Digital craftspeople: learn the tools used to build Tope-là's digital creations, adapt them, and provide technical information that influences other collaborators' decisions
skillsOutro: Each of these roles has varying expectations in time and skills, co-created between collaborators so expectations are clear, realistic for each situation, and relevant to Tope-là's mission.
compensationTitle: An important word on contribution and compensation
compensationP1: At this stage of the project, contributions to Tope-là are not paid in euros. The goal is first to build a solid framework, tools, and collective dynamic.
compensationP2: Forms of compensation may emerge later - in euros or through other Tope-là-specific mechanisms - but there is no certainty or promise on this point.
compensationP3: Everyone is therefore invited to commit only at a level that is fair and sustainable for them, consciously.
contactLead: If you feel a pull toward this context and think you check many of these boxes, write to us at
